name: From La Villajoyosa to the Embalse d'Amodorio via the Volta del Cantal (PR-CV 437)
name_original: Desde La Villajoyosa hasta el Embalse d'Amodorio por la Volta del Cantal (PR-CV 437)
region_intro: Hiking around La Vila Joiosa features a varied landscape, combining undeveloped coastal stretches with accessible mountainous terrain. The region is characterized by dramatic cliffs, serene coves, and views of the Amadorio River and its reservoir. Hikers can explore a mix of coastal paths, river valleys, and more challenging mountain routes. Best hiking trails around La Vila Joiosa The most popular hiking route is From La Villajoyosa to the Embalse d'Amodorio via the Volta del Cantal (PR-CV 437), an 8.4 miles (13.4 km) trail that takes 3 hours 38 minutes to complete. This moderate route leads to the Amadorio Reservoir. Another top favourite among local hikers is View of Racó del Conill Cove – View of Villajoyosa loop from Castellum Romano, a moderate 5.6 miles (9.0 km) path. This route offers views of Racó del Conill Cove and the surrounding area. Local hikers also love the View of Villajoyosa – Aguiló Tower loop from Hospital Vila, a 6.7 miles (10.7 km) trail leading through coastal landscapes, often completed in about 3 hours 9 minutes. Hiking around La Vila Joiosa is defined by diverse coastal paths, river and reservoir views, and varied elevation gains. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy strolls to more challenging treks. The routes in La Vila Joiosa are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 500 reviews. More than 3,000 hikers have used komoot to explore La Vila Joiosa's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in La Vila Joiosa?
A: La Vila Joiosa offers a wide variety of hiking experiences, with over 110 routes documented on komoot. These range from easy strolls to more challenging mountain treks, ensuring options for all fitness levels.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on hikes in La Vila Joiosa?
A: The region features a diverse landscape, combining undeveloped coastal stretches with accessible mountainous terrain. You'll find dramatic cliffs, serene turquoise coves, and views of the Amadorio River and its reservoir. Trails can lead through coastal paths, river valleys, and more rugged mountain areas.

Q: Are there easy hiking trails suitable for families or beginners?
A: Yes, La Vila Joiosa has over 60 easy routes perfect for families or those seeking a relaxed walk. A great option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31401346" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Amadorio Reservoir – Amadorio Reservoir loop from Parc Observatori Recreatiu El Pantà&lt;/a&gt;, which offers beautiful views of the reservoir on well-maintained paths. The urban walk, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/27629713" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Villajoyosa Beach – Villajoyosa Old Town loop from la Vila Joiosa&lt;/a&gt;, is also an easy and scenic choice.

Q: Can I find circular hiking routes in La Vila Joiosa?
A: Many of the hiking routes in La Vila Joiosa are designed as loops, allowing you to start and end at the same point. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10329619" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Racó del Conill Cove – View of Villajoyosa loop from Castellum Romano&lt;/a&gt; offers stunning coastal vistas, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10329379" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Villajoyosa – Aguiló Tower loop from Hospital Vila&lt;/a&gt; explores coastal landscapes and historical towers.

Q: What notable landmarks or viewpoints can I see while hiking?
A: Hiking in La Vila Joiosa offers opportunities to discover various landmarks and viewpoints. Along coastal paths, you might encounter historical watchtowers like the Torre de Sant Josep or the Aguiló Tower. Inland, you can find natural features such as the Amadorio Reservoir. For impressive mountain views, consider routes near &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/497288" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puig Campana Summit&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3576092" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cabeçó d'Or Summit&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any challenging hikes for experienced hikers?
A: Yes, for experienced hikers seeking more strenuous activities, La Vila Joiosa and its surroundings offer challenging options. There are 4 difficult routes available, and the region includes mountain passes and peaks like El Puigcampana, which provides significant elevation gain for mountaineering experiences. Trails such as the PR-CV-289 Loop also offer more demanding terrain.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in La Vila Joiosa?
A: The hiking routes in La Vila Joiosa are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 500 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse coastal paths, the scenic views of the Amadorio River and reservoir, and the varied elevation gains that cater to different ability levels.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in La Vila Joiosa?
A: Given its Mediterranean climate, the best time for hiking in La Vila Joiosa is typically during the cooler months, from autumn through spring (October to May). During these periods, temperatures are more moderate, making longer hikes more comfortable. Summer can be very hot, so early morning or late afternoon hikes are recommended if visiting then.

Q: Are the hiking trails in La Vila Joiosa dog-friendly?
A: Many natural areas and trails in La Vila Joiosa are generally dog-friendly, especially those away from crowded urban beaches. However, it's always advisable to keep your dog on a leash and check for specific local regulations or signage on individual trails, particularly in protected areas or during certain seasons. Ensure you carry enough water for both yourself and your pet.

Q: Are there any hikes that include opportunities for wild swimming?
A: La Vila Joiosa is known for its pristine coastline and numerous coves. While specific 'wild swimming' spots on hiking trails aren't explicitly marked, many coastal paths, such as those leading to Racó del Conill or Cala del Xarco, offer direct access to the sea. These coves are ideal for a refreshing dip after a walk, especially during warmer months.

Q: Is parking available at the trailheads for hiking routes?
A: Parking availability varies by trailhead. For routes starting near urban areas or popular beaches, you can often find public parking, though it might be limited during peak season. For trails leading into more natural or remote areas, parking might be less formal. It's recommended to check the starting point of your chosen route on komoot for specific parking information or local advice.

Q: Can I access hiking trails using public transport?
A: While La Vila Joiosa has public transport options within the town and connecting to nearby areas, direct access to all trailheads via public transport can be limited, especially for routes in more rural or mountainous regions. Some coastal paths and urban walks are easily accessible, but for others, a short taxi ride or personal vehicle might be necessary to reach the starting point.
tips: The reservoir is known as the closest to the sea in all of Spain. It is an alternative site to beach tourism in the region of La Marina Baja and has several alternative hiking routes to explore or reach it. It is recommended to bring water since there are no nearby sources and in summer the heat is very hot!
